title: Introduction to Dance Studies (3) A&H/SSc
catalogDescription: Introduces students to reading and writing dance scholarship, including study of how cultural, social, historical, aesthetic, and political issues intersect with dance practices in a variety of global contexts. Provides the foundation for upper-level dance-studies courses. Includes lectures, reading, writing, watching dance, discussion, and movement explorations.

title: Introduction to Contemporary Dance Making
catalogDescription: Introduction to fundamental tools of making dance in a contemporary context to access creative and expressive potential and practice composing, choreographing, and directing.

title: Embodied Rhythms
catalogDescription: Introduction to basic music theory, rhythmic embodiment, polyrhythmic drumming, and analysis of of musical patterns as they manifest in a variety of music and dance genres.

catalogDescription: Dance majors explore their strengths and interests in real world career options. Includes guest professionals, cover letter and resume development, and job shadowing. Offered: W.

title: Dance Teaching Methodologies
catalogDescription: Introduction to dance pedagogy, including educational theory, motor learning, and biomechanical principles and music as it relates to the teaching of dance. Recommended: DANCE 242 and DANCE 493. Offered: W.
prerequisiteNotes: Official UW recommended-preparation text: DANCE 242 and DANCE 493.

title: Graduate Dance Composition
catalogDescription: Addresses creative process, directing, and critical analysis in dance composition. Focus on interdisciplinary collaborative processes. Emphasizes learning about creative processes that extend across disciplines, while creating a common language. Through a variety of collaborative studies encourages creative exploration and development of ideas from the conceptual stage to working models. Focuses on process, not product. Offered: A.

title: Master's Project
catalogDescription: Project in area of interest developed in consultation with faculty adviser and supported by elective courses. Full faculty approval of proposed project by end of first year. Formal presentation, appropriate to project's content, presented to full faculty during second year. Project culminates in the teaching of an undergraduate dance course.
